notes: SCHEMA EXAMPLE. Each object is one golden query (the answer key for one retrieval query). Fields: 'query' (the user text, required); 'intent' (required, one of: genre|person|year|country|paraphrastic|semantic); 'relevant_titles' (required, >=1 genuinely-correct titles — aim for >=3); 'distractors' (optional, plausible-but-wrong neighbours the corpus must also contain so ranking is actually tested); 'notes' (optional, free text). Titles are resolved to jellyfin_ids at load time via LibraryStore — each must match exactly one library item.
A 'country' intent example. Requires corpus NFOs to carry <country> metadata.

query: something cozy for a rainy night
notes: A 'paraphrastic' example. These cases are REPORTED but EXCLUDED from the regression gate because the LLM rewrite step is non-deterministic.
